Url.description: The endpoint to which you establish a websocket connection.

BatchChannelMemberships.description: The list of channel memberships in the response.

Errors.description: If the action fails for one or more of the memberships in the request, a list of the memberships is returned, along with error codes and error messages.

MemberArn.description: The AppInstanceUserArn of the member that the service couldn't add.

CallbackId.description: The call back ID passed in the request.

ChannelArn.description: The ARN of the response to the ban request.

Member.description: The ChannelArn and BannedIdentity of the member in the ban response.
The ARN and metadata of the member being added.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel in the response.

ChannelModerator.description: The ARNs of the channel and the moderator.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time at which the AppInstanceUser created the channel.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which a member sent the last message in the channel.

LastUpdatedTimestamp.description: The time at which a channel was last updated.

ElasticChannelConfiguration.description: The attributes required to configure and create an elastic channel. An elastic channel can support a maximum of 1-million members.

MaximumSubChannels.description: The maximum number of SubChannels that you want to allow in the elastic channel.

TargetMembershipsPerSubChannel.description: The maximum number of members allowed in a SubChannel.

MinimumMembershipPercentage.description: The minimum allowed percentage of TargetMembershipsPerSubChannel users. Ceil of the calculated value is used in balancing members among SubChannels of the elastic channel.

ExpirationSettings.description: Settings that control when a channel expires.

ExpirationDays.description: The period in days after which the system automatically deletes a channel.

ExpirationCriterion.description: The conditions that must be met for a channel to expire.

Member.description: The member being banned from the channel.

ChannelArn.description: The ARN of the channel from which a member is being banned.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time at which the ban was created.
The time at which the channel flow was created.

LastUpdatedTimestamp.description: The time at which a channel flow was updated.

InvitedBy.description: The identifier of the member who invited another member.

Type.description: The membership type set for the channel member.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time at which the channel membership was created.

LastUpdatedTimestamp.description: The time at which a channel membership was last updated.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel that a user belongs to.

ChannelMembership.description: The channel to which a user belongs.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which the last persistent message visible to the caller in a channel was sent.

AppInstanceUserMembershipSummary.description: Returns the channel membership data for an AppInstance.

ReadMarkerTimestamp.description: The time at which an AppInstanceUser last marked a channel as read.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel that the AppInstanceUser is a member of.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which the last persistent message visible to the caller in a channel was sent.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time at which the moderator was created.

ChannelMessage.description: The details of and content in the message.

Content.description: The content of the channel message. For Amazon Lex V2 bot responses, this field holds a list of messages originating from the bot. For more information, refer to Processing responses from an AppInstanceBot in the Amazon Chime SDK Messaging Developer Guide.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time at which the message was created.

LastEditedTimestamp.description: The time at which a message was edited.

LastUpdatedTimestamp.description: The time at which a message was updated.

Persistence.description: The persistence setting for a channel message.

Detail.description: Contains more details about the message status.

MessageAttributes.description: The attributes for the channel message. For Amazon Lex V2 bot responses, the attributes are mapped to specific fields from the bot. For more information, refer to Processing responses from an AppInstanceBot in the Amazon Chime SDK Messaging Developer Guide.

ContentType.description: The content type of the channel message. For Amazon Lex V2 bot responses, the content type is application/amz-chime-lex-msgs for success responses and application/amz-chime-lex-error for failure responses. For more information, refer to Processing responses from an AppInstanceBot in the Amazon Chime SDK Messaging Developer Guide.

Target.description: The target of a message, a sender, a user, or a bot. Only the target and the sender can view targeted messages. Only users who can see targeted messages can take actions on them. However, administrators can delete targeted messages that they can’t see.

Detail.description: Contains more details about the message status.

ResourceArn.description: The ARN of the resource in the configuration.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested bans are returned.

Member.description: The member being banned from a channel.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested channels are returned.

ChannelMemberships.description: The information for the requested channel memberships.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested channel memberships are returned.

ChannelMemberships.description: The information for the requested channel memberships.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested users are returned.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which the last persistent message visible to the caller in a channel was sent.

AppInstanceUserMembershipSummary.description: Returns the channel membership data for an AppInstance.

ReadMarkerTimestamp.description: The time at which an AppInstanceUser last marked a channel as read.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel that the AppInstanceUser is a member of.

ChannelArn.description: The ARN of the channel containing the requested messages.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested messages are returned.

ChannelMessages.description: The information about, and content of, each requested message.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel in the response.

Content.description: The content of the channel message. For Amazon Lex V2 bot responses, this field holds a list of messages originating from the bot. For more information, refer to Processing responses from an AppInstanceBot in the Amazon Chime SDK Messaging Developer Guide.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time at which the message summary was created.

LastUpdatedTimestamp.description: The time at which a message was last updated.

LastEditedTimestamp.description: The time at which a message was last edited.

Status.description: The message status. The status value is SENT for messages sent to a channel without a channel flow. For channels associated with channel flow, the value determines the processing stage.

Detail.description: Contains more details about the message status.

MessageAttributes.description: The attributes for the channel message. For Amazon Lex V2 bot responses, the attributes are mapped to specific fields from the bot. For more information, refer to Processing responses from an AppInstanceBot in the Amazon Chime SDK Messaging Developer Guide.

ContentType.description: The content type of the channel message listed in the summary. For Amazon Lex V2 bot responses, the content type is application/amz-chime-lex-msgs for success responses and application/amz-chime-lex-error for failure responses. For more information, refer to Processing responses from an AppInstanceBot in the Amazon Chime SDK Messaging Developer Guide.

Target.description: The target of a message, a sender, a user, or a bot. Only the target and the sender can view targeted messages. Only users who can see targeted messages can take actions on them. However, administrators can delete targeted messages that they can’t see.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested moderators are returned.

ChannelModerators.description: The information about and names of each moderator.

NextToken.description: The token returned from previous API requests until the number of channels is reached.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which the last persistent message visible to the caller in a channel was sent.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested channels are returned.
The token returned from previous API requests until the number of channels moderated by the user is reached.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which the last persistent message visible to the caller in a channel was sent.

NextToken.description: The token passed by previous API calls until all requested sub-channels are returned.

MembershipCount.description: The number of members in a SubChannel.

ExpirationSettings.description: Settings that control the interval after which a channel is deleted.

ExpirationDays.description: The period in days after which the system automatically deletes a channel.

ExpirationCriterion.description: The conditions that must be met for a channel to expire.

Preferences.description: The ARN and metadata of the member being added.

ResourceArn.description: The ARN of the resource in the configuration.

ChannelArn.description: The ARN of the channel containing the messages that you want to redact.

MessageId.description: The ID of the message being redacted.

SubChannelId.description:
The ID of the SubChannel in the response.

Only required when redacting messages in a SubChannel that the user belongs to.

Channels.description: A list of the channels in the request.

NextToken.description: The token returned from previous API responses until the number of channels is reached.

LastMessageTimestamp.description: The time at which the last persistent message visible to the caller in a channel was sent.

MessageId.description: The ID string assigned to each message.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel in the response.

Detail.description: Contains more details about the message status.

MessageId.description: The ID string of the message being updated.

SubChannelId.description: The ID of the SubChannel in the response.

Detail.description: Contains more details about the message status.
